OEE.definition: Overall Equipment Effectiveness - a manufacturing metric that measures production efficiency by combining availability, performance, and quality factors into a single percentage.

translations.th: ประสิทธิผลโดยรวมของอุปกรณ์

languageNotes.fr: Use 'TRS' (Taux de Rendement Synthetique) instead of OEE in French

availability.definition: The percentage of planned production time that the equipment was actually producing. Per ISO 22400 (E.1.1), calculated as Production Time (Operating Time) divided by Planned Time. Reduced by all stops within planned time -- both unplanned stops and planned stops included in OEE. A key component of OEE.

translations.th: อัตราความพร้อมใช้งาน

examples: Machine availability was 85% due to unplanned breakdowns
Planned downtime for maintenance affects availability
Calculate availability: 420 operating minutes / 480 planned minutes = 87.5%

performance.definition: The ratio of actual production speed to ideal production speed during operating time. Per ISO 22400 (E.1.2), measures how efficiently the equipment runs when it is running. Accounts for speed losses and minor stops that reduce output below the theoretical maximum.

translations.th: ประสิทธิภาพ

examples: Performance dropped to 75% due to frequent minor stoppages
Actual speed was 80 units/hour vs ideal of 100 units/hour = 80% performance
Slow cycles and idle time reduce the performance score

quality.definition: The percentage of good units produced out of total units, excluding scrap and rework. Per ISO 22400 (E.1.3), calculated as Good Quantity divided by Total Quantity. Reflects first-pass yield and measures how many products meet specifications without defects.

translations.th: คุณภาพ

examples: Quality was 98% with only 20 rejected units out of 1000 produced
First-pass quality excludes reworked parts from the good count
Quality issues during startup caused a 5% quality loss

cycle time.definition: The time required to produce one unit of product from start to finish. Used as the basis for calculating production speed and the Performance component of OEE. Comparing actual cycle time to ideal cycle time reveals speed losses.

translations.th: เวลาวงจร

examples: The cycle time for this part is 45 seconds
Reducing cycle time by 5 seconds increased daily output by 200 units
Actual cycle time vs ideal cycle time determines speed loss

ideal cycle time.definition: The theoretical minimum time required to produce one unit under optimal conditions, representing the fastest sustainable production rate. Used as the baseline denominator for Performance calculations. Should be validated and updated when equipment or processes change.

translations.th: รอบเวลาอุดมคติ
translations.vi: thời gian chu kỳ lý tưởng

examples: Ideal cycle time of 30 seconds per unit was validated during time study
Performance = Ideal Cycle Time × Total Pieces / Operating Time
Update ideal cycle time after equipment upgrades

station.definition: A production workstation or machine where manufacturing operations occur. The fundamental unit for tracking production data in Evocon. Each station has its own OEE metrics, shift schedules, and product configurations.

translations.th: สถานีงาน

examples: The packaging station has an OEE target of 75%
Configure production speed settings for each station
Stations can be grouped by production line or factory
station.domainDisambiguation: Manufacturing workstation where production occurs. NOT transit station (train/bus), NOT radio station.

factory.definition: A production facility containing one or more stations. Used for organizational grouping and access control.

translations.th: โรงงาน

product.definition: An item being manufactured at a station. Products have specific speed settings and can be tracked through production orders.

translations.th: ผลิตภัณฑ์

operator.definition: A person who works at a station during a shift, responsible for running equipment and logging production data. Operators interact with the system to register stops, add comments, complete checklists, and report quality issues.

translations.th: ผู้ปฏิบัติการ

examples: The operator logged a breakdown stop with comments
Assign operators to shifts for accurate performance tracking
Operators complete quality checklists at product changeover

shift.definition: A defined work period during which production occurs and operators are assigned. Shifts have start and end times and serve as the primary time boundary for production reporting. Performance metrics are typically analyzed per shift.

translations.th: กะการทำงาน

examples: The morning shift runs from 6:00 to 14:00
Shift handover includes reviewing stop reasons from previous shift
Compare OEE across different shifts to identify best practices
shift.domainDisambiguation: A scheduled work period for production (e.g. 8-hour manufacturing shift). NOT keyboard shift key, NOT gear shift.

downtime.definition: Periods when production is stopped and the equipment is not operating. Per EN 13306 (5.8), the time interval during which an item is in a down state. Includes both planned downtime (scheduled maintenance, breaks) and unplanned downtime (breakdowns, material shortages). Also referred to as 'stops' in the UI.

translations.th: เวลาหยุดเครื่อง

examples: Unplanned downtime from breakdowns totaled 2 hours today
Planned maintenance downtime is scheduled weekly
Downtime tracking helps identify recurring equipment issues
downtime.domainDisambiguation: Period when equipment is not operating due to failure, maintenance, or other disruption. NOT general "free time" or "break".

stop.definition: A single instance of production stoppage. Can be planned (scheduled maintenance, breaks) or unplanned (breakdowns, material shortage).

translations.th: การหยุด

stop.domainDisambiguation: A production stoppage event with a reason code. NOT a bus stop or punctuation mark.

stop reason.definition: The categorized cause for a production stop, selected by operators or automatically assigned. Stop reasons are organized hierarchically and used for Pareto analysis to identify the biggest sources of downtime. Essential for continuous improvement initiatives.

translations.th: สาเหตุการหยุดเครื่อง

examples: The operator selected 'Material shortage' as the stop reason
Top stop reasons this week: Breakdown (40%), Changeover (30%)
Stop reasons are grouped into categories for analysis

changeover.definition: The transition period when switching production from one product to another, including setup and adjustment. In Evocon, changeovers can trigger checklists automatically and are tracked as stop events. Changeover stops can be configured as either included in or excluded from OEE calculations.

translations.th: การเปลี่ยนรุ่นการผลิต

examples: Changeover from Product A to Product B took 45 minutes
Quick changeover techniques reduced setup time by 60%
Changeover is typically excluded from availability calculations
changeover.domainDisambiguation: Process of switching production from one product to another, including setup and adjustment. NOT a generic "change" or "transition".

scrap.definition: Defective or rejected production units that do not meet quality standards. Scrap directly reduces the Quality component of OEE, calculated as scrap quantity divided by total quantity. Scrap is tracked with scrap reasons for root cause analysis.

translations.th: ของเสีย

examples: 15 units were scrapped due to dimensional errors
Scrap rate of 2% means Quality is at maximum 98%
Record scrap at the end of each production run

scrap reason.definition: The categorized cause for why units were scrapped. Used to track and analyze quality issues.

translations.th: สาเหตุของเสีย

good quantity.definition: The number of units produced that meet quality standards. Calculated as Total Quantity minus Scrap.

translations.th: จำนวนชิ้นดี

total quantity.definition: The total number of units produced, including both good units and scrap.

translations.th: ปริมาณรวม

speed loss.definition: Production running slower than the ideal cycle time, reducing the Performance component of OEE. Speed losses occur when machines run below optimal speed due to wear, material issues, or operator intervention. Distinct from stops, which halt production entirely.

translations.th: การสูญเสียความเร็ว

examples: Speed loss of 20% due to worn tooling
Material quality issues caused the line to slow down
Speed loss reasons help identify performance bottlenecks

target speed.definition: The expected or goal production speed for a product at a station. Used to measure performance against expectations.

translations.th: ความเร็วเป้าหมาย

production speed.definition: The actual rate at which units are being produced, typically measured in units per minute or per hour.

translations.th: ความเร็วในการผลิต

operating time.definition: The time during which the equipment is actually running and producing output, comprising both good production time (at or above ideal speed) and slow production time (below ideal speed). Equivalent to Planned Time minus all downtime within planned time. This is the numerator in the Availability calculation.

translations.th: เวลาเดินเครื่อง

examples: 8 hours planned time - 1 hour downtime = 7 hours operating time
Operating time increased after reducing changeover durations
Track operating time to measure actual productive hours
operating time.domainDisambiguation: Time during which equipment is actively producing output. NOT employee working hours or shift duration.

planned time.definition: The time scheduled for production during a shift, excluding planned stops that are configured as 'not included in OEE' (such as scheduled breaks or maintenance). Calculated as Shift Time minus Planned Stops Excluded from OEE. This is the denominator in the Availability calculation. Planned time is narrower than shift time.

translations.th: เวลาที่วางแผนไว้

examples: The shift has 8 hours of planned production time
Planned time excludes scheduled maintenance windows
Availability = Operating Time / Planned Time × 100%

setup time.definition: Time spent preparing equipment for production, including changeovers and initial configuration.

translations.th: เวลาเตรียมเครื่อง

setup time.domainDisambiguation: Time spent preparing/reconfiguring equipment for a new product run (changeover). NOT software installation time.

duration.definition: The length of time for an event such as a stop, shift, or production run.

translations.th: ระยะเวลา

dashboard.definition: A customizable view displaying widgets with production metrics, charts, and KPIs. The main interface for monitoring performance.

translations.th: แดชบอร์ด

Shift View.definition: The operator-facing interface for viewing and managing the current shift's production data, stops, and checklists.

translations.th: มุมมองกะ

checklist.definition: A set of tasks or checks that operators must complete, typically at regular intervals or triggered by events like changeovers.

translations.th: รายการตรวจสอบ

extra shift.definition: An additional shift created outside the normal shift template schedule, used for overtime, special production runs, or one-off scheduling needs. Unlike regular shifts generated from templates, extra shifts are manually defined for specific dates and times.

translations.th: กะเพิ่มเติม

production signal.definition: A discrete event recorded when equipment produces output, marking the production of a specific quantity at a timestamp. Each signal carries a quantity value and optional notes. Signals are the fundamental data points from which cycle time and production speed are calculated.

translations.th: สัญญาณการผลิต

production signals.definition: Multiple production signal events. Used when referring to the list or management of production signals in the system. Each signal is a discrete event marking the production of a specific quantity at a timestamp.

translations.th: สัญญาณการผลิต

shift template.definition: A reusable schedule pattern that defines the recurring start and end times for production shifts. Shift templates are configured in settings and applied to stations to automatically generate shift schedules. Each template can define multiple shifts per day with specific start/end times and days of the week.

translations.th: แม่แบบกะการทำงาน

shift time.definition: The total scheduled duration of a shift, calculated as Planned Time plus Planned Stops Excluded from OEE. Shift time represents the full work period including all scheduled activities (production, breaks, maintenance). It is broader than Planned Time and is used as the denominator in OOE calculations.

translations.th: เวลากะ

shift times.definition: The scheduled durations of shifts. Used when referring to the start and end times of shifts across stations, particularly for matching or comparing shift schedules between stations.

translations.th: เวลากะ

speed loss reason.definition: The categorized cause for production running slower than the ideal cycle time, selected by operators to document why speed losses occurred. Speed loss reasons are used for Pareto analysis to identify the biggest sources of performance loss.

translations.th: สาเหตุการสูญเสียความเร็ว
translations.vi: nguyên nhân tổn thất tốc độ

stop reasons.definition: The categorized causes for production stops, organized hierarchically and used for Pareto analysis to identify the biggest sources of downtime. Used when referring to the list or management of all stop reason entries in the system.

translations.th: สาเหตุการหยุดเครื่อง

technical availability.definition: The percentage of planned time not lost to technical stops (equipment failures and breakdowns). Per ISO 22400 (E.1.1) and EN 13306 (5.1), calculated as 1 minus (Technical Stop Time divided by Planned Time). Unlike general Availability which considers all downtime, Technical Availability isolates only stops classified as technical failures, providing a focused view of equipment reliability.

translations.th: ความพร้อมใช้งานทางเทคนิค

OOE.definition: Overall Operations Effectiveness - measures effectiveness across the total operating time (or shift time). Unlike OEE, OOE includes all downtime (both planned and unplanned) that occurs during hours when the plant is scheduled to work, providing a broader view of how effectively scheduled time is used.

translations.th: OOE

TEEP.definition: Total Effective Equipment Performance - measures effectiveness across all available time (24 hours/7 days a week, 365 days a year). TEEP reveals the 'hidden factory' or maximum theoretical potential capacity by showing how much of the total calendar time is used productively.

translations.th: TEEP

OEE calculation.definition: The method of computing Overall Equipment Effectiveness by multiplying Availability x Performance x Quality. Each component is a percentage, and the result represents the overall productive efficiency of a station or production line.

translations.th: การคำนวณ OEE

examples: OEE calculation: 90% availability x 85% performance x 98% quality = 75% OEE
The OEE calculation excludes planned stops that are marked as excluded from OEE

loss.definition: A production loss - any factor that reduces the effectiveness of a station from its theoretical maximum output. In Evocon, losses are categorized as availability losses (stops/downtime), performance losses (speed reductions), and quality losses (scrap/defects). Not to be confused with generic 'loss' - this always refers to manufacturing production losses.

translations.th: การสูญเสีย

examples: The biggest loss this week was unplanned downtime
Speed loss accounted for 15% of total losses
Quality loss from scrap reduced OEE by 2%
loss.domainDisambiguation: Reduction in equipment effectiveness due to stops, speed reductions, or quality defects. NOT financial loss or emotional loss.

good production.definition: Production output that meets quality standards, used in two related senses: (1) as a time measure, the duration during which equipment was producing at or above ideal cycle time (as opposed to slow production); and (2) as an output measure, the usable production excluding scrap. In time usage reports, good production time plus slow production time equals operating time. The separate term 'good quantity' refers specifically to the count of units meeting quality standards.

translations.th: ผลผลิตดี

stop type.definition: The classification of a production stop. Stop types include: planned (scheduled maintenance, breaks), unplanned (breakdowns, failures), planned included in OEE (counted against availability), and planned excluded from OEE (not counted against availability). Stop type determines how the stop affects OEE calculations.

translations.th: ประเภทการหยุดเครื่อง

examples: Set the stop type to 'planned excluded' for scheduled lunch breaks
Unplanned stop types always count against OEE availability

unplanned stops.definition: Production stops that were not scheduled or expected, such as equipment breakdowns, material shortages, or quality issues. Unplanned stops always reduce OEE availability and are a primary target for continuous improvement.

translations.th: การหยุดเครื่องนอกแผน

planned stops.definition: Production stops that are scheduled in advance, such as maintenance, breaks, or changeovers. Planned stops can be configured as either included in OEE (reducing availability) or excluded from OEE (not affecting availability), depending on how the organization defines planned production time.

translations.th: การหยุดเครื่องตามแผน

examples: Planned stops (incl. in OEE) reduce the availability component
Planned stops (excl. from OEE) are subtracted from total time before calculating OEE

uncommented.definition: A status indicating that a production stop or event has not yet been commented on by an operator. Uncommented stops require attention as they lack context about the root cause or corrective action taken.

translations.th: ยังไม่มีความเห็น

batch.definition: A defined quantity of product manufactured in a single production run under uniform conditions. Batches are tracked for quality, traceability, and production planning purposes.

translations.th: ล็อตการผลิต

examples: The current batch of 500 units is expected to complete by end of shift
Each batch is assigned a unique lot number for traceability
batch.domainDisambiguation: A specific production run of a product, tracked for quality and traceability. NOT a generic group or collection.

product code.definition: A unique identifier assigned to each product in the system, used to distinguish between different items being manufactured. Product codes are used for product selection, reporting, and production tracking.

translations.th: รหัสผลิตภัณฑ์

LOT/Batch number.definition: A unique tracking identifier assigned to a specific production batch or lot. Used for traceability, quality control, and regulatory compliance to track products through the manufacturing process.

translations.th: LOT/หมายเลขล็อต

primary unit.definition: The main unit of measure used for counting production output at a station, such as pieces, kilograms, or meters. The primary unit is used in OEE calculations and production speed settings.

translations.th: หน่วยหลัก

alternative unit.definition: A secondary unit of measure for production output, used alongside the primary unit. For example, if the primary unit is pieces, the alternative unit might be kilograms or pallets, allowing production to be tracked in multiple units simultaneously.

translations.th: หน่วยทางเลือก

location.definition: A named location within a station where an individual machine operates. Translate as 'location' (not 'position'). A single station (e.g., a bottling line) consists of multiple locations such as bottling, labeling, and capping - each identifying a distinct machine within that line.

translations.th: สถานที่

locations.definition: Multiple machine locations within a station. Translate as 'locations' (not 'positions'). Used when referring to the list or management of all individual machines that make up a station or production line.

translations.th: สถานที่

machine location.definition: The location of an individual machine within a station. Translate as 'machine location' (not 'machine position'). For example, on a bottling line station, the labeling machine's location identifies where it sits in the production sequence. Machine locations enable tracking and managing individual machines that together form a complete station.

translations.th: ตำแหน่งเครื่องจักร

machine locations.definition: Multiple individual machine locations within a station. Translate as 'machine locations' (not 'machine positions'). Used when referring to the list or management of all machines that make up a station or production line.

translations.th: ที่ตั้งเครื่องจักร

stations.definition: Multiple production workstations or machines. Used when referring to lists, groups, or management of multiple stations in the system.

translations.th: สถานีทำงาน

station groups.definition: Organizational groupings of stations, typically by production line, area, or function. Station groups allow aggregated reporting and management of related machines.

translations.th: กลุ่มสถานี

checklists.definition: The feature name for the collection of checklist forms in the system. Checklists are sets of tasks or checks that operators complete at regular intervals or triggered by events.

translations.th: รายการตรวจสอบ

checklist groups.definition: Organizational groupings of related checklists. Checklist groups help manage and categorize checklists by purpose, area, or frequency.

translations.th: กลุ่มรายการตรวจสอบ
translations.vi: các nhóm danh sách kiểm tra

Factory Overview.definition: A view in the application that provides a high-level summary of all stations and their current status within a factory. Shows real-time production metrics across the entire factory floor.

translations.th: ภาพรวมโรงงาน

Grid View.definition: A view layout that displays stations or data in a grid format, allowing users to see multiple stations side by side for quick comparison of production metrics.

translations.th: มุมมองตาราง

extra note.definition: An additional free-text note field that can be attached to production events, stops, or shifts. Extra notes provide supplementary context beyond standard stop reasons or comments.

translations.th: หมายเหตุเพิ่มเติม

downtime auto-commenting.definition: A feature that automatically generates comments for downtime events based on predefined rules, reducing the manual effort required from operators while ensuring all stops are documented.

translations.es: comentario automático de tiempos de paro
translations.sq: komentimi automatik i kohëve të ndaljes
translations.ro: comentare automată a timpului de nefuncționare
translations.th: การแสดงความเห็นอัตโนมัติสำหรับเวลาหยุดเครื่อง
translations.vi: tự động ghi chú thời gian dừng máy

No shift days.definition: Calendar days where no production shifts are scheduled. No shift days are excluded from production time calculations and reporting to avoid skewing metrics.

translations.th: วันไม่มีกะ

empty shift.definition: A scheduled shift during which no production data was recorded. Empty shifts may indicate equipment idle time, missing data entry, or periods where the station was not in use despite being scheduled.

translations.th: กะว่าง

empty shift reason.definition: A reason code explaining why a shift had no production data. Empty shift reasons help distinguish between intentional idle time and data collection issues.

translations.th: เหตุผลกะว่าง

speed loss reasons.definition: The categorized causes for production running slower than the ideal cycle time. Used when referring to the list or management of all speed loss reason entries in the system.

translations.th: สาเหตุการสูญเสียความเร็ว
translations.vi: nguyên nhân tổn thất tốc độ

speed loss groups.definition: Organizational groupings of speed loss reasons by category or cause type. Speed loss groups enable aggregated analysis of performance losses.

translations.th: กลุ่มการสูญเสียความเร็ว

default scrap reason.definition: A pre-configured scrap reason that is automatically assigned when scrap is recorded without a specific reason selected. Ensures all scrap events have at least a baseline categorization.

translations.th: สาเหตุของเสียเริ่มต้น
translations.vi: lý do phế phẩm mặc định

default scrap reason.domainDisambiguation: Pre-configured fallback scrap reason used when no specific reason is selected. "Default" means pre-configured fallback, NOT standard/typical.

product changeover reason.definition: The reason given for why a product changeover was performed, such as a new production order, customer request, or schedule change. Helps track and analyze the causes of changeover events.

translations.th: เหตุผลการเปลี่ยนผลิตภัณฑ์
translations.vi: lý do chuyển đổi sản phẩm

company administrator.definition: A user role with the highest level of administrative access across all factories in a company. Company administrators can manage users, configure global settings, and access data across the entire organization.

translations.th: ผู้ดูแลระบบบริษัท

factory administrator.definition: A user role with administrative access scoped to a specific factory. Factory administrators can manage station settings, user permissions, and configurations within their assigned factory.

translations.th: ผู้ดูแลระบบโรงงาน
